channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Considerable number of dumped carrier bags, 14th July
item.description:
Lots of dumped bags and cardboard box next to full and overflowing bin. Bin was full yesterday morning.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): High Road, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Trees requires pruning between 14 and 16 Malle Road, 14th July
item.description:
The trees between 14 and 16 Maple Road require trimming, particularly around the lower branches. We have reported this issue previously, but no action has been taken by the council. The tree in front of No. 16 has become overgrown near the ground, and the dense foliage is being used to conceal dog waste and litter, making the area unsightly and difficult to keep clean. In addition, during periods of strong wind, branches have fallen onto nearby properties and plants. There is also a risk that falling branches could injure pedestrians using the pavement, so we would appreciate the trees being inspected and pruned as soon as possible.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 14, Maple Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped mattress, 14th July
item.description:
Pls remove

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Jenner Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox, 14th July
item.description:
Dead fox in middle of the pavement
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mackenzie Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox in road, 14th July
item.description:
There is a young dead fox outside 65 Stone Park Avenue in the main road - killed overnight
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stone Park Avenue, Eden Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Yet again…. Huge amount of waste left on road, 14th July
item.description:
Please help us with this - daily, people fly tip and leave waste at this corner. Please plant some more trees or add a bench to try and change this behaviour. Please. This is unsafe. Today, there was food - I saw rats yesterday - as well as lots of other abandoned furniture, toys all left here. It grew throughout the day. As more people left other waste.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cobourg Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Over grown trees branches, 14th July
item.description:
You can't see the bus stop as the trees branches are covering it

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Beulah Hill, Upper Norwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Deflated bouncy castle or dinghy in the water where the ducks are, 14th July
item.description:
Deflated bouncy castle or dinghy in the water where the ducks aRe. What 3 words location is codes.points.edits

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Five Arches, North Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: More rubbish left, 14th July
item.description:
Building materials, DIY materials and also a desk. This is a repeated weekly issue. Please install a camera !

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Royal Hill, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Ground-floor wheelie bins repeatedly left uncollected at Edgar House, 14th July
item.description:
Despite repeated reports since December 2025, this issue remains unresolved. Residents should not have to repeatedly report the same issue to receive a basic council service they already pay for.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Homerton Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lack of street shading, empty tree pits, 14th July
item.description:
This street has several empty tree pits which would benefit from replacement planting. This would provide much needed shade and improve the look of the street. The trees were removed less than three years ago and never replaced. There are at least two empty pits on this side of the street. The position of the trees, when they were alive, was such that they provided shade during the hottest part of the day when houses on that side of the street get direct and sustained sunlight. Additionally as the pavements have no soakaways or ground level planting, the footpath is all concrete which further raises and radiates heat. Without any shade this makes our houses even hotter and unmanageable in hot weather.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Worbeck Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Block 125 to 135, 14th July
item.description:
I reported this Friday 10/07/2026 I received a text message saying the issue has been resolved clearly it has not so I’m assuming there is miscommunication between departments at hackneys cleanliness department and crews They deploy to help clear it up

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Victorian Grove, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet
